name: Cash Store
description_short: Cash Store Tomball, Texas — Cash Store offers unsecured installment loans with same-day funding at physical locations. Serves customers with bad or no c...
description_long:
Cash Store operates a network of retail lending locations, including the Houston location at 19734 Tomball Pkwy. The company specializes in unsecured installment loans designed for customers who may not qualify for traditional bank financing. Their Houston location (Store #30796) maintains extended hours (10am-6pm weekdays, 10am-2pm Saturday) and advertises same-day funding capabilities.

The company's primary product is installment loans—unsecured personal loans repaid over a fixed schedule—which customers can use for major purchases, bill consolidation, or unexpected expenses. They also offer Netspend Prepaid Mastercard accounts and retail refinancing/reload options for existing customers. The application process involves online pre-qualification followed by in-store documentation review and underwriting, with funds typically disbursed the same day upon approval.

Cash Store distinguishes itself by explicitly stating they consider applicants with bad or no credit, positioning themselves as an alternative to traditional lenders. Their website emphasizes a streamlined three-step process and highlights customer service quality through Google reviews (4.9 stars from 212 reviews). Staff members are individually named and praised for professionalism and helpfulness across recent customer testimonials.

However, the company provides no transparent disclosure of interest rates, fees, or APR ranges on their website. Loan terms, maximum amounts, and actual approval rates remain unstated. While marketed as an alternative for credit-challenged borrowers, the lack of rate transparency prevents consumers from understanding the true cost of borrowing before applying. The reliance on in-store applications (despite online pre-qualification) creates friction compared to fully digital lenders.

As a financial institution, this lender competes with both traditional banks and newer fintech personal loan lenders in the consumer lending space. Borrowers seeking personal loans for bad credit may find more flexible terms through online lenders, while those focused on simplifying payments may benefit from debt consolidation loans with fixed rates. For credit building, secured credit cards and credit builder loans offer structured paths to improvement. Credit monitoring services provide ongoing visibility into credit health, and credit counseling through nonprofit agencies can help consumers create sustainable budgeting plans. Many of these lenders offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments over 12 to 60 months, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Same-day funding available upon approval and loan acceptance
Accepts applicants with bad or no credit history
Extended hours (10am-6pm weekdays, 10am-2pm Saturday) support working customers
Only requires 30-day-old checking account, not extensive banking history
High Google review rating (4.9/5 from 212 recent reviews)
Staff personally named and praised for professionalism and patience in reviews
Simple three-step process: pre-qualify online, gather documents, complete in-store
cons: No APR, interest rates, or fee schedules disclosed on website
No maximum or minimum loan amounts published
Requires in-store visit despite online pre-qualification option
Website mentions system maintenance issues (Check 'n Go site down)
No information on approval rates or typical terms offered
best_for: Borrowers with bad credit or no credit history seeking personal loans
Customers needing funds within one business day in the Houston area
People without traditional banking relationships seeking installment loans
Unbanked or underbanked consumers with checking accounts less than 30 days old
diagnosis: Cash Store is best for credit-challenged borrowers in the Houston area who need personal loans with same-day funding and can visit a physical location. The critical caveat is the complete absence of published interest rates and fees—applicants cannot determine the true cost of borrowing before committing to an in-store application, making rate comparison impossible.
services: Unsecured installment loans for major purchases or bill consolidation
Loan refinancing for existing retail customers
